mechanism_narrative: Low and stable consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) provides a favorable macroeconomic environment, reducing uncertainty and supporting corporate profit margins, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL is below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to reduced consumer spending and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 40% of triggered instances following a significant rise in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Robust growth in retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicates healthy consumer demand and investment, boosting corporate earnings expectations across various sectors and driving positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are flat or negative in more than 55% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) during a monetary tightening cycle increases the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances during identified monetary tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in real GDP (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which typically translates into higher corporate revenues and profits, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are consistently negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) during periods of positive GDPC1 growth over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant month-over-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown, negatively impacting corporate earnings and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a significant INDPRO decline.

mechanism_narrative: High employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with a low unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, supporting strong consumer spending and corporate revenues, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat (average return < 0.01%) when both PAYEMS growth is strong and UNRATE is low.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) in an environment of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) stimulate construction and related industries, signaling broad economic strength and contributing to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are negative despite strong HOUST when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5% in more than 50% of triggered instances.
